steps.title: Understand what the blank must do
steps.content: The first clause says Iceland's volcanoes are extreme outliers because their magma comes from nearly 3,000 km deep. The second clause explains why they're outliers: most volcanoes are fed by shallow magma less than 15 km down. The blank must connect these two clauses with a transition that shows the second clause confirms or elaborates on the first.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong transitions, keep the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'indeed,': “Indeed” signals confirming elaboration — the second clause provides evidence that Iceland is an outlier by showing the norm it departs from. ✓
  • Choice B — 'nevertheless,': Violates transition logic — “nevertheless” signals a contrast or concession, but the second clause agrees with and supports the first rather than contradicting it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'in addition,': Violates transition logic — “in addition” introduces a new, separate idea, but the second clause elaborates on the same point (why Iceland is an outlier), not an unrelated fact. ✗
  • Choice D — 'consequently,': Violates transition logic — “consequently” signals a cause-and-effect result, but Iceland's outlier status does not cause other volcanoes to use shallow magma. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
